items.headline: Indonesia moves capital operations to Nusantara after decades of planning
items.body: Indonesia officially began relocating government ministries to Nusantara, its new capital in East Kalimantan, this week. The shift redistributes political and economic gravity away from overcrowded Jakarta, affecting infrastructure priorities across the archipelago.

items.headline: Ecuador and Peru agree to shared Amazon watershed protection zone
items.body: Ecuador and Peru signed a binding agreement creating a jointly managed conservation corridor covering 14 million hectares of Amazon rainforest. The pact sets a precedent for cross-border resource governance in a region where extraction rights have historically triggered conflict.

items.headline: Saudi Arabia opens labour market to women without male guardian approval
items.body: Saudi authorities removed the requirement for women to obtain male guardian consent to sign employment contracts, effective immediately. The reform expands economic autonomy for millions and accelerates the kingdom's workforce restructuring ahead of oil revenue decline.

items.headline: African Union launches continental pharmaceutical manufacturing initiative
items.body: The African Union inaugurated a pooled procurement and production framework enabling member states to jointly manufacture essential medicines and vaccines. The system reduces import dependence that left the continent waiting years for pandemic supplies.
